description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with two stacked wings supported by four sets of struts on each side, giving it a classic early 20th-century appearance. The fuselage is streamlined and painted in a muted gray-blue color, with a rounded nose and a wooden propeller mounted at the front. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, featuring a single seat and visible flight controls. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer with rounded tips. Subtle red accent lines trace the edges of the wings and tail, adding a touch of detail to the otherwise simple color scheme.
description: A classic biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear, finished in gray-blue with red accents.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the dual stacked wings, tail assembly, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open cockpit, fixed landing gear, and the layered wing structure with supporting struts.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the wooden propeller, rounded nose, and the symmetrical biplane wing arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit, landing gear, and the tail skid, with clear visibility of the wing struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; rounded tips on both surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a fixed tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
